hero.name: Ahmed Saad
hero.role: PCB Design Engineer | Electronics Hardware Designer
hero.desc: I design reliable electronic circuits and professional PCB layouts for prototypes, embedded systems, and custom hardware projects.

about.title: About Me
about.headline: Aspiring Hardware Designer focusing on reliable physical manufacturing.
about.body: I am an Electrical Engineering student and aspiring PCB Design Engineer with a strong interest in electronics, circuit design, embedded systems, and hardware development. I focus on creating organized, manufacturable, and reliable PCB designs while continuously improving my technical and practical skills through rigorous hands-on laboratory work and industry-standard simulation studies.

timeline.title: B.Sc. in Electrical Engineering
timeline.desc: Focusing on Electronics, Signals & Systems, Power Systems, and Embedded microcontrollers. Actively participated in robotics and hardware clubs.

timeline.title: Advanced PCB Design Course
timeline.desc: Studied high-speed PCB routing guidelines, signal integrity, power distribution networks (PDN), thermal management, and design for manufacturing (DFM).

timeline.title: Embedded Hardware Bootcamp
timeline.desc: Hands-on experience in schematic capturing, footprint creation, routing double and multi-layer boards using Altium and KiCad.

items.title: Schematic Circuit Design
items.desc: Transforming technical ideas into structured and simulated circuit diagrams with proper noise filtering and electrical rules.

items.title: PCB Layout Design
items.desc: Professional positioning of electronic parts and routing traces with optimal impedance matching and noise minimization.

items.title: Single and Multi-Layer PCB Design
items.desc: Layout solutions for up to 4 layers focusing on compact spacing, signal crosstalk prevention, and solid power planes.

items.title: Component Selection
items.desc: Selecting high-efficiency, cost-effective, and highly available components avoiding supply chain depletion or obsolete parts.

items.title: PCB Routing and Optimization
items.desc: Routing with strict current density limits, preventing thermal hotspots, and reducing high-frequency emission lines.

items.title: Design Rule Check (DRC)
items.desc: Rigorous execution of physical design rule checks against manufacturer limitations to guarantee 100% production yields.

eda.name: KiCad EDA
eda.desc: Main design tool for full schematic capture, library creation, and multi-layer layout routing.

eda.name: Altium Designer
eda.desc: Industrial projects, impedance control calculation, and interactive 3D board verification.

eda.name: EasyEDA
eda.desc: Rapid client prototyping, direct integration with LCSC parts catalog and JLCPCB fabrication.

sim.name: LTspice
sim.desc: Analog circuit simulations, transient response checks, noise evaluation before committing to layouts.

sim.name: Proteus
sim.desc: Interactive digital circuit simulation and microcontrollers register code validation.

steps.title: Understanding Requirements
steps.desc: Discussing key technical specs: voltage, input/output signals, dimensional limits, and mechanical clearances with the client.

steps.title: Circuit Planning
steps.desc: Creating high-level block diagrams to isolate power stages, signal paths, MCU zones, and protection circuits.

steps.title: Schematic Capture
steps.desc: Building the full electrical schematic with verified component symbols, net labels, and simulation-ready connections.

steps.title: Component Sourcing
steps.desc: Selecting available, cost-effective parts while screening for lifecycle risk and footprint accuracy.

steps.title: PCB Layout & Routing
steps.desc: Placing components and routing copper traces with controlled impedance and thermal considerations.

steps.title: Design Review (DRC)
steps.desc: Running complete industrial Design Rule Checks to confirm zero overlapping, sufficient spacings, and correct mask thresholds.

steps.title: Generating Manufacturing Files
steps.desc: Exporting standard Gerber X2 formats, Excellon drill files, Bill of Materials (BOM) tables, and pick-and-place coordinate data.

steps.title: Final Delivery & Support
steps.desc: Delivering fully zipped fabrication archives, providing professional assembly support, and explaining test procedures.

items.text: Ahmed designed an extremely reliable multi-channel sensor hub for our laboratory. The ground plane segmentation successfully cleared high-frequency noise from our ADC readings. Amazing communication and strict attention to our DRC guidelines.
items.name: Dr. Khaled Al-Mansoor

items.text: Outstanding results. The Gerbers Ahmed provided loaded flawlessly into the manufacturer portal with zero design exceptions. He helped choose standard cost-effective parts that saved us 15% in direct assembly costs.
items.name: Omar Al-Farsi

items.text: Excellent thermal layout! Even under continuous heavy loads of 8A, the heat remains well-distributed across the vias and copper floods as predicted in his simulation studies. A very reliable PCB designer indeed.
items.name: Sarah Connor

items.q: What information do you need to start a PCB project?
items.a: I need your schematic or a clear description of the circuit's function, target voltage/current, board size constraints, and desired layer count. Sketches, reference designs, or datasheets for key components are very helpful too.

items.q: Which PCB design software do you use?
items.a: I primarily work in KiCad for full designs, with Altium Designer for industrial-grade impedance-controlled projects, and EasyEDA for quick prototyping with direct JLCPCB integration.
Yes. Every completed project includes standard Gerber X2 files, Excellon drill files, a Bill of Materials (BOM), and pick-and-place coordinates ready for manufacturing.

items.q: Can you review an existing PCB design?
items.a: Absolutely. I offer design review services covering DRC compliance, thermal analysis, signal integrity, and manufacturability improvements.
Yes, I design 2-layer and 4-layer boards with controlled impedance routing, proper ground plane isolation, and via optimization for high-speed signals.

items.q: Do you provide the source files?
items.a: Yes, native KiCad/Altium project files are included with every delivery so you retain full ownership and editability of your design.

contact.desc: Have a custom PCB design, schematic diagram, or hardware debugging issue in mind? Submit your detailed specs, target constraints, or sketches, and I will get back to you with a comprehensive technical quote in less than 24 hours.
